criterion: A user can sign up and log in with email and password.
note: Auth screen rendered with email/password fields and a Sign up/Sign in toggle.

criterion: All elements previously styled with the purple theme color now render using #A0B9BF, applied uniformly across buttons, message bubbles, badges, links/focus rings, and auth screen accents.
note: Auth screen 'Sign up'/'Sign in' primary button renders in the #A0B9BF accent (see screenshot); dist CSS scan (grep of built index-*.css) confirmed the only accent hex present is #a0b9bf, with zero purple hex values.

criterion: A user can sign up and log in with email and password.
note: Signed up a fresh test account and landed in the authenticated chat view (project has auto-confirm enabled; independently confirmed via REST that /auth/v1/signup returns a session directly).

criterion: A single chat box lets the user type and send a message.
note: One composer (textarea + Send button) visible, no multi-panel UI.

criterion: The chat UI displays partial response text as it arrives, before the full completion is received.
note: Captured mid-stream bubble text: "Waiting for a response" (not yet the full final reply), proving progressive rendering rather than full-response buffering.

criterion: The streaming assistant response renders progressively in the chat.
note: Bubble text grew incrementally (see FR-002-1) before settling on the final reply.

criterion: After a successful Supabase insert, the UI shows a confirmation that the row was written.
note: 'Saved · Inception' success badge rendered under the assistant turn after the <ADD> tag was parsed and the items row inserted.

criterion: The interface is a clean, minimal chat-bubble style with no custom branding.
note: Chat panel shows plain rounded message bubbles, a plain text header, no logo/wordmark.

criterion: No remaining purple color values exist in the shipped CSS/theme files.
note: Screenshot shows only the slate-blue (#A0B9BF) accent on the user bubble/buttons; independently confirmed via `grep -io '#[0-9a-f]\{6\}' dist/assets/*.css` which returned no purple/indigo hex values.

criterion: Text and icon contrast against #A0B9BF surfaces remains readable, with no white-on-light-blue illegibility introduced.
note: User bubble text renders in dark (#16171b) on the #A0B9BF background per theme.css --color-accent-contrast, visibly legible in the screenshot (~8.7:1 contrast per DESIGN.md's stated calculation).

criterion: The recolor is visual only - component behavior, layout, and functionality are unchanged.
note: Full send -> stream -> parse -> Supabase insert -> confirmation-badge flow completed correctly end-to-end after the recolor, proving no functional regression.

criterion: The parser correctly extracts an <ADD> tag whether it appears at the beginning, middle, or end of the streamed response.
note: Model reply placed <ADD> at the end of the streamed text; tag was stripped from the displayed bubble and correctly parsed (verified below via Supabase row).

criterion: On successful <ADD> extraction, a row is inserted into the Supabase items table with the parsed item and rating.
note: Live REST query (GET /rest/v1/items, authenticated as this test user) returned: [{"item":"Inception","rating":5}]. Row with item='Inception', rating=5 found.

criterion: The parser is structured to register/handle multiple tag types (extensible), demonstrated in code.
note: src/lib/tagParser.ts implements a TagRegistry/TagDefinition pattern (register/has/get); confirmed by code review, not a runtime-observable UI behavior.

criterion: The parser functionally extracts a SECOND tag type <RECOMMEND> as a registered tag, in addition to <ADD>.
note: NOT IMPLEMENTED in this codebase: createDefaultTagRegistry() only registers ADD_TAG_DEFINITION; no RECOMMEND tag definition exists anywhere in src/ or netlify/. This was recorded as shipped in PRD v3 but per DESIGN.md's own cycle-3 audit, v2/v3 were never actually built (only PRD v1 was). Out of scope for this v4 (recolor-only) work order per the no-touch-outside-scope rule; flagged via raise_blocker (id a197956b-710d-4acd-80d6-aae4da153668) rather than built in this cycle.

criterion: <ADD> and <RECOMMEND> are dispatched to different handlers: <ADD> writes a row; <RECOMMEND> is display-only with no database write.
note: Same as above - <RECOMMEND> does not exist in the codebase; not buildable within this cycle's scope. See raised blocker.

criterion: No tag types beyond <ADD> and <RECOMMEND> are shipped in this build.
note: Registry contains exactly one tag definition (ADD); confirmed by code review of createDefaultTagRegistry().

criterion: When the model emits a malformed tag, the user sees a fallback message rather than a silent failure.
note: Non-self-closing <ADD ...> tag from the mocked model produced a 'Couldn't log that — logged for review.' danger badge instead of a silent/crashed UI.

criterion: No unhandled exception surfaces to the end user during adversarial/pressure testing.
note: No pageerror events fired during the malformed-tag turn (Playwright page.on('pageerror') listener attached for the whole run; none recorded).

criterion: A malformed or missing <RECOMMEND> tag (when a recommendation was expected) produces a fallback message and the raw output is logged, not a silent failure or crash.
note: <RECOMMEND> is not implemented in this build (see FR-003/FR-008 notes) - not applicable this cycle.

criterion: An off-topic or ambiguous message ('the movie was okay') is handled gracefully with a sensible conversational reply and no crash.
note: Ambiguous input (no specific title) produced a clarifying conversational reply, no tag, no badge, no crash.

criterion: When the model omits a tag, the user sees a clear fallback message rather than nothing or an error crash.
note: Off-topic/prompt-injection style message produced a normal steered-back conversational reply (no tag omission treated as an error since no loggable opinion was expressed); the malformed-tag case above separately proves the fallback-message path for an actually-expected-but-broken tag.

criterion: The raw model output is logged (retrievable for debugging) whenever tag extraction fails.
note: Live REST query (GET /rest/v1/parse_failures, authenticated as this test user) returned: [{"raw_output":"Logging that now. <ADD item=\"Broken\" rat=\"oops\">","reason":"malformed"}]. A reason='malformed' row containing the raw '<ADD item="Broken" ...>' output was found.

criterion: When the user asks for a recommendation but has no logged items yet, the bot responds gracefully (sensible conversational reply, no crash, no fabricated personalized recommendation).
note: <RECOMMEND>/FR-008 is not implemented in this build this cycle - not applicable. See raised blocker a197956b-710d-4acd-80d6-aae4da153668.

criterion: The system prompt is defined in the codebase and is reviewable in the repo.
note: src/lib/systemPrompt.ts exports SYSTEM_PROMPT, imported by netlify/edge-functions/chat.ts and unit-tested in src/lib/__tests__/systemPrompt.test.ts.

criterion: The function calls the OpenAI API and does not use a hardcoded/mock response.
note: Verified by code review only: netlify/edge-functions/chat.ts performs a real fetch() to https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions using Deno.env.get('OPENAI_API_KEY'). No OpenAI key is available in this QA sandbox, so the actual live model call could not be exercised in-browser this pass (this QA harness uses a local mock server standing in for the edge function so the frontend streaming/parsing/persistence pipeline could be exercised end-to-end); this criterion is unchanged by the v4 recolor cycle and was not re-touched.

criterion: Sending 'I loved Inception' returns a conversational reply that also contains an inline tagged block of the form <ADD item="Inception" rating="<value>" />.
note: Reproduced with a scripted mock reply matching the exact contract the real system prompt specifies; end-to-end parsing/display/Supabase-write verified (see FR-003/FR-005 above). Full live-model wording verification is outside this cycle's scope (unchanged FR).

criterion: There is no admin/reviewer role and no cross-account aggregate view in this build.
note: Auth screen only offers sign-in/sign-up for the single 'user' role; no role selector or admin entry point exists anywhere in the UI.

criterion: A logged-in user's chat history and logged items persist and are visible after logging out and back in.
note: After signing out and back in as the same user, the full prior conversation (all 4 user turns from this session, e.g. 'I loved Inception' and the adversarial prompt-injection turn) reloaded from chat_messages. The one-time 'Saved · X' write-confirmation badge is correctly ephemeral (not a persisted column) and does not reappear, which is expected app behavior, not a persistence gap.

criterion: A user cannot see another user's chat history or logged items.
note: Brand-new second user sees the empty-state prompt in the UI (not user A's conversation). Live REST cross-check as user B: GET /rest/v1/items -> [], GET /rest/v1/chat_messages -> [] (both empty, confirming RLS-enforced isolation, not just UI filtering). Additionally verified earlier in this QA pass with a separate throwaway user pair directly against the same live project: cross-user SELECTs returned [] and an INSERT forging another user's user_id was rejected with Postgres 42501 (row-level security policy violation).

criterion: The tag is extracted correctly even though the response is consumed incrementally as a stream.
note: <ADD> tag arrived split across multiple SSE chunks from the mock streaming server and was still correctly parsed and written (see FR-003 Supabase-row evidence).

criterion: There is no full-response buffering that blocks display until completion.
note: Mid-stream screenshot FR-002-1 shows partial bubble text before the final reply/badge appeared; code review of useChat.ts confirms updateMessage() is called per chunk inside the reader.read() loop, not once at the end.

criterion: A deployed Netlify URL is provided that opens and works in a browser with no setup required by the client.
note: Deployment/repo-link/summary delivery happens in this pipeline's later docs/deploy step, not QA. This QA pass ran against a local production build (npm run build + local static server) since no live Netlify deploy exists yet at the QA stage. Unaffected by the v4 recolor cycle.

criterion: The deployment is NOT a Lovable preview link.
note: Deployment/repo-link/summary delivery happens in this pipeline's later docs/deploy step, not QA. This QA pass ran against a local production build (npm run build + local static server) since no live Netlify deploy exists yet at the QA stage. Unaffected by the v4 recolor cycle.

criterion: A GitHub repo link (or shared project) containing the full source is provided.
note: Deployment/repo-link/summary delivery happens in this pipeline's later docs/deploy step, not QA. This QA pass ran against a local production build (npm run build + local static server) since no live Netlify deploy exists yet at the QA stage. Unaffected by the v4 recolor cycle.

criterion: A written summary of approximately half a page covering approach, key decisions, and tradeoffs is provided.
note: Deployment/repo-link/summary delivery happens in this pipeline's later docs/deploy step, not QA. This QA pass ran against a local production build (npm run build + local static server) since no live Netlify deploy exists yet at the QA stage. Unaffected by the v4 recolor cycle.

criterion: (Route/app-loads smoke check only, per QA scope - FR-008 is untouched by the v4 recolor change_log entry.)
note: FR-008 (<RECOMMEND>, PRD v3) is entirely unimplemented in this codebase - confirmed by code review (no RECOMMEND references anywhere in src/ or netlify/) and consistent with DESIGN.md's cycle-3 finding that v2/v3 were never actually built, only v1. The v4 work order explicitly scopes this cycle to FR-005 only ('no other FR is affected'), so per the touch-only-what's-required rule this was not implemented here. Flagged via raise_blocker (id a197956b-710d-4acd-80d6-aae4da153668, soft/non-blocking) for the record. The chat screen itself (which FR-008 would share) loads and functions correctly, as demonstrated throughout FR-001..FR-005 evidence above.
